[2018-03-11 21:24] mqp: it's possible i would have to change plans because opponent would go DDC into DDB threatening redeemer with xeno/redeemer plan
[2018-03-11 21:25] mtanzer: it looks like a tricolor set where you get to 7 attack somewhat early with kinetic driver.  if villain doesn't also kinetic driver you can get an uncountered arka
[2018-03-11 21:25] mtanzer: probably also want a xeno guardian
[2018-03-11 21:25] mtanzer: oh and don't get redeemer first or it just enables their arka
[2018-03-11 21:27] p0lari (Polari): actually shouldn't the early redeemer be fine if you also get a kinetic out?
[2018-03-11 21:28] p0lari (Polari): I hadn't thought about that before
[2018-03-11 21:28] mtanzer: yeah I guess it's not nearly as bad since you can do an early snipe, but still wouldn't you rather not have to do an early snipe?
[2018-03-11 21:29] p0lari (Polari): idk, people keep telling me it's better than I think
[2018-03-11 21:30] p0lari (Polari): if you get kinetic as first attacker then redeemer next turn, you get four out of six turns of attack out of it
[2018-03-11 21:31] mtanzer: yeah actually that sounds fine
[2018-03-11 21:31] mtanzer: redeemer before kinetic still sounds meh
[2018-03-11 21:32] p0lari (Polari): even that seems fine? it's still not that bad of a gold trade, and getting animus and double forge that early should be pretty awkward for them
[2018-03-11 21:32] mtanzer: I've done it a bunch of times, it's not really that awkward imo
[2018-03-11 21:33] p0lari (Polari): I mean of course it's totally worth it if you do get the arka
[2018-03-11 21:33] awaclus: a lot of the time Kinetic is worth it just for the threat
[2018-03-11 21:33] mtanzer: if they snipe something you also don't have too much tech either right?
[2018-03-11 21:34] mtanzer: besides, there's trinity and xeno.  I think having BBA tech a few turns after your opponent buys redeemer seems fine
[2018-03-11 21:36] p0lari (Polari): hmm yeah, if you get the kinetic first then the goose timing is one turn later than with the early redeemer, and the tech purchases will be spread out
[2018-03-11 21:36] p0lari (Polari): maybe they could even get a triple forge and golden armor animus for the arka turn
[2018-03-11 21:36] mtanzer: charges a turn later and kinetic a turn earlier so you're getting 2 more attacks with kinetic
[2018-03-11 21:37] mtanzer: plus kinetic as a first attacker over redeemer seems good anyway (although I'm not sure if xeno changes things)
[2018-03-11 21:37] p0lari (Polari): right, I just mean, surely we've all played games where someone misses arka and gets a normal redeemer
[2018-03-11 21:37] mtanzer: yeah if they really wanted to force the arka they could triple forge.  I think triple forge is mediocre in that set?  but I've underestimated apollo plenty before
[2018-03-11 21:37] p0lari (Polari): I'm just thinking about this line compared to how those games play out
[2018-03-11 21:39] p0lari (Polari): yeah I don't think triple forge is *great*, but if it guarantees arka through your kinetic?
[2018-03-11 21:39] p0lari (Polari): I'm probably getting too deep into theorycrafting here
[2018-03-11 21:40] mtanzer: no I think we're doing the right amount of theorycrafting 😃
[2018-03-11 21:40] p0lari (Polari): I bet if I actually tried out this stuff in analysis something would be totally off and none of this would work
[2018-03-11 21:41] mtanzer: even if that's true, I'm sure there's gonna be some arka/kinetic sets where choices like this do exist
[2018-03-11 21:41] mtanzer: I guess arka/kinetic/redeemer is the combination in question
[2018-03-11 21:47] gex__ (gex): Any tips against Redeemer? Especially when you don't have natural conduit?
[2018-03-11 21:48] Deleted User (Deleted User): unfortunately it was an autowin set for me =/
[2018-03-11 21:48] gex__ (gex): Are you TheTrumpWall?
[2018-03-11 21:49] mtanzer: if they get redeemer first just get a wall
[2018-03-11 21:49] mtanzer: try to set up your own redeemer a bit later, you don't need to rush it out
[2018-03-11 21:50] mtanzer: the turn the gauss charges arrive is a popular time to buy your response redeemer
[2018-03-11 21:52] p0lari (Polari): the fast p2 redeemer isn't that great if there's nothing else to support it
[2018-03-11 21:52] gex__ (gex): Hmm, but the opponent buys another redeemer and I only have 1 blue. I would have to buy another blastforge, which seems inefficient..
[2018-03-11 21:52] p0lari (Polari): get a wall to absorb 2/3 of its damage, get a little ahead on econ, then get your own redeemer on the gauss charge turn
[2018-03-11 21:52] p0lari (Polari): on the other hand if there is good support in pool it can make the set hugely p2 favoured
[2018-03-11 21:52] gex__ (gex): http://play.prismata.net/?r=wsUZc-ofsik
[2018-03-11 21:53] gex__ (gex): Yeah, there was Barrier, which seems to help P2 alot
[2018-03-11 21:54] wiwiweb: A question I've been asking myself: If you're breachproof and you don't care about syncing, is it better to buy Isos or Gauss cannons
[2018-03-11 21:54] p0lari (Polari): these gauss cannons seem pretty bad to me, and so does killing galvanis instead of engis
[2018-03-11 21:54] p0lari (Polari): double forge is good in redeemer games
[2018-03-11 21:54] wiwiweb: the :Gold: saved is not worth the extra buildtime?
[2018-03-11 21:55] p0lari (Polari): with gauss you get the damage earlier
[2018-03-11 21:55] gex__ (gex): Yeah, the short time control did not help, basically just panicking and buying whatever.
[2018-03-11 21:56] p0lari (Polari): I haven't analysed the math as closely as others, but I've been told gauss is more efficient (assuming all of the damage is relevant) and that matches my intuition
[2018-03-11 21:56] Deleted User (Deleted User): yes I am TheTrumpWall

[2018-03-12 22:35] gadget246: what qualities in a set cause you to consider going breachproof, and what might the early turns of a breachproof strat look like?
[2018-03-12 22:35] Deleted User (Deleted User): breachproof units, lack of good attack/defense
[2018-03-12 22:35] Deleted User (Deleted User): early turns, spam econ
[2018-03-12 22:37] redzonex (Redzone): you pretty much need a way of having breachproof economy
[2018-03-12 22:37] 307th: yeah, usually you need a way of turning your drones into something breachproof - thorium dynamo, trinity drone, or venge cannon, as well as a breachproof attacker
[2018-03-12 22:38] 307th: and on top of that like cooledcannon says breachproof won't be good if the normal attack/defense in the set is strong
[2018-03-12 22:38] 307th: i would say thorium dynamo is the #1 breachproof unit
[2018-03-12 22:39] redzonex (Redzone): having good green sinks like thorium, venge, or CLUSTERBOLT is helpful too

[2018-03-13 00:31] gadget246: is it possible to use auric impulses and/or thorium dynamos early to get your economy up faster than DD/DDE/DDD ?  and if so how?
[2018-03-13 00:32] 307th: thorium dynamo yes, auric impulses no
[2018-03-13 00:32] crash_overlord: Auric is a drone you sell the next turn. It actually slows down "economy" but can speed up big unit purchases.
[2018-03-13 00:33] 307th: yeah, the benefit of auric is that you can 'sell' the drone, the downside is that you end up down an energy (since you get 4 gold next turn, not 4 gold and an energy)
[2018-03-13 00:34] 307th: thorium dynamo is slightly more efficient than regular drone, as long as you don't waste any energy with it
[2018-03-13 00:34] 307th: so, the standard dynamo opening for p1 is 1. DD 2. D1 (where 1 is dynamo) 3. DD
[2018-03-13 00:34] 307th: for p2 it's 1. DD 2. 11 3. D
[2018-03-13 00:35] gadget246: perfect, that's super helpful, thanks
[2018-03-13 00:44] Deleted User (Deleted User): @307th the problem with your article's premise is I believe a full value botch is always better than a tarsier which costs a bt(no absorb denial)
[2018-03-13 00:45] 307th: i did mention grimbotch is higher value overall, but there are times when tarsier > botch
[2018-03-13 00:45] Deleted User (Deleted User): your article implies you might not want to get full value botch
[2018-03-13 00:47] 307th: i think that there are cases where you'd prefer tarsier over full value botch, even post-absorb, although they're rare
[2018-03-13 00:47] 307th: e.g. an antima situation, or other situations where defense is plentiful
[2018-03-13 00:47] 307th: but more to the point there are lots of situations early on , even post-absorb, when you'd rather tarsier over botch simply because botch won't get full value
[2018-03-13 00:48] 307th: so i think the article won't lead people to misplay botch too much
[2018-03-13 00:48] 307th: i might go over the article a bit later and double check the phrasing though
[2018-03-13 00:49] Deleted User (Deleted User): Yeah, true, esp @ post-antima and not always getting full value
[2018-03-13 00:49] Deleted User (Deleted User): I haven't done the math, but I believe punf did and if I remembered correctly, no defence is cheap enough for full value botch to ever be worse
[2018-03-13 00:49] mtanzer: I'm kinda with trump on this, I think a botch that just swings 4 times is as good as a tarsier already.  haven't read the article yet
[2018-03-13 00:50] Deleted User (Deleted User): Like, your article is more technically correct I think
[2018-03-13 00:50] mtanzer: Same as how I think a fission that doesn't get clicked is also usually good
[2018-03-13 00:50] Deleted User (Deleted User): but a simple flippant Punf-rule of "always get full value botch" would make your plays much stronger than trying to analyse every nuance
[2018-03-13 00:50] 307th: this article is more aimed at getting people to not make 2 sentinels as their first attackers
[2018-03-13 00:51] mtanzer: lemme read the article now 😃
[2018-03-13 00:51] 307th: or, to put that another way, i think you guys are focusing on the fringe cases where this article's advice doesn't apply
[2018-03-13 00:51] Deleted User (Deleted User): it's probably less elegant because botch and tarsier are both 4R, but the point is probably better with tarsier/rhino
[2018-03-13 00:51] 307th: but i think in most cases this article's correct
[2018-03-13 00:51] 307th: and you just aren't noticing that because you take it for granted due to how much experience you have
[2018-03-13 00:52] Deleted User (Deleted User): using botch as an example might lead players to underbuy botch, when it's a ridic OP unit
[2018-03-13 00:52] Deleted User (Deleted User): relative to tarsier anyway
[2018-03-13 00:52] 307th: i do say that it's better than tarsier
[2018-03-13 00:53] mtanzer: after skimming some of it I'm just worried the article tries to emphasize the pros and cons of short-term units vs. long-term units when the truth is a lot of short-term units are just good purchases all the time even if the game will be long
[2018-03-13 00:54] gadget246: unrelated: it seems to me that you can transfer back and forth between auric impulses and engineers to generate a lot of extra defense on a crippled economy in a breachproof strat. but it might just be that the bot is bad and let me do it when a player wouldn't
[2018-03-13 00:54] mtanzer: like I don't even distinguish units that are short-term vs. long-term in my decision making usually.  If I have a choice between kinetic and goose I'll always pick kinetic because it's the better unit
[2018-03-13 00:54] mtanzer: unless there's some exception like I'm getting breached very soon

[2018-03-13 00:56] mtanzer: yeah I think I can agree to that example.  but I also think you kinda have to go to that extreme (tarsier being way better than rhino in long-term value) to see it in action
[2018-03-13 00:58] Deleted User (Deleted User): you don't actually want defense in a bp strat- but 1-2 E/aurics aren't bad generally
